description:
What: Grep all HeapKind::Float allocation sites. RawF64 in FieldKind already handles typed paths. If all remaining sites are polymorphic-edge re-boxes, delete the rest. If HeapKind::Float becomes unreachable, delete the variant.

Why: Confirm typed paths skip boxing; remove duplication if any exists.

CLIF impact: removes one alloc_float per typed-float return site, if any remain.

Removes: up to one HeapKind variant + its allocator (audit-gated).

Tests: audit notes attached to ticket; deletions covered by existing float tests.

comments.text:
**Audit complete — no deletions warranted.**

Live HeapKind::Float allocation sites found:
- `src/ir_codegen.rs:6036` — float literal in Tagged context (any-typed slot). Required: float literal can't stay raw when its consumer is polymorphic.
- `src/ir_codegen.rs:6117` — slow path for BinOp on tagged-typed floats (the polymorphic arithmetic fallback after typed fast-path miss). Required: `slow_arith` closure must box result via fz_alloc_float for the tagged return shape.
- `src/ir_codegen.rs:6976` — `tag_box_float` helper, canonical RawF64→Tagged promotion. Required by every polymorphic edge.
- `runtime/src/ir_runtime.rs:830,1007,1093` — runtime polymorphic-edge re-boxes (fz_alloc_float extern + FFI promotion paths).

Every site is a polymorphic-edge re-box exactly as the ticket hypothesized. The typed RawF64 fast paths (`try_typed_binop_fast_path`, frame-slot RawF64 entries) already skip these. **HeapKind::Float variant remains live and necessary** for the polymorphic edge.

Recommendation: close as audit-complete, no code changes.

title: fz-dps.E1: audit + delete dead HeapKind::Float alloc sites
